Interface Backend

All Known Implementing Classes:
InMemoryBackend
public interface Backend
Abstraction over the server-side storage and delivery of chat messages.

A Backend is responsible for reserving and releasing nicknames and for delivering public and private messages to connected clients. Implementations may choose different storage strategies; a simple in-memory implementation is provided by chat.server.InMemoryBackend.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    broadcast(String fromNick, String text)
    Broadcasts a public message to all connected clients.
    void
    broadcastUsersList()
    Broadcasts the current users list to all connected clients.
    void
    releaseNick(String nick)
    Releases a previously reserved nickname.
    boolean
    reserveNick(String nick)
    Attempts to reserve a nickname for a connecting client.
    boolean
    sendPrivate(String fromNick, String toNick, String text)
    Sends a private message to a specific recipient.
    String
    usersCsv()
    Returns the list of reserved nicknames as a comma-separated string.

  • Method Details

    • reserveNick

      boolean reserveNick(String nick)
      Attempts to reserve a nickname for a connecting client.
      Parameters:
      nick - the requested nickname
      Returns:
      true if successful, false if the nickname is already taken

    • releaseNick

      void releaseNick(String nick)
      Releases a previously reserved nickname. Implementations should be idempotent.
      Parameters:
      nick - the nickname to release

    • broadcast

      void broadcast(String fromNick, String text)
      Broadcasts a public message to all connected clients.
      Parameters:
      fromNick - sender nickname
      text - message text

    • sendPrivate

      boolean sendPrivate(String fromNick, String toNick, String text)
      Sends a private message to a specific recipient.
      Parameters:
      fromNick - sender nickname
      toNick - recipient nickname
      text - message text
      Returns:
      true if the receiver exists and the message was queued for delivery; false otherwise

    • usersCsv

      String usersCsv()
      Returns the list of reserved nicknames as a comma-separated string.
      Returns:
      e.g., "alice,bob"; empty string if there are no users

    • broadcastUsersList

      void broadcastUsersList()
      Broadcasts the current users list to all connected clients.
